content: According to Ezra 1:1, of which kingdom is Cyrus king?
explanation: Ezra 1:1 — 'In the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that the word of the LORD by the mouth of Jeremiah might be fulfilled, the LORD stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia...'

content: According to Ezra 1:1, in which year of Cyrus's reign was the decree issued?
explanation: Ezra 1:1 — 'In the first year of Cyrus king of Persia... the LORD stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, so that he made a proclamation throughout all his kingdom...'

content: According to Ezra 1:2, what did Cyrus declare God had charged him to build, and where?
explanation: Ezra 1:2 — '...The LORD, the God of heaven, has given me all the kingdoms of the earth, and he has charged me to build him a house at Jerusalem, which is in Judah.'

content: According to Ezra 1:7, what did Cyrus restore to the people of Israel?
explanation: Ezra 1:7 — 'Cyrus the king also brought out the vessels of the house of the LORD that Nebuchadnezzar had carried away from Jerusalem and placed in the house of his gods.'
options: Vessels of the house of the LORD that Nebuchadnezzar had carried away

content: According to Ezra 1:8, to whom did Cyrus deliver the vessels for return to Judah?
explanation: Ezra 1:8 — 'Cyrus king of Persia brought these out in the charge of Mithredath the treasurer, who counted them out to Sheshbazzar the prince of Judah.'

content: According to Ezra 2:64, what was the total number of the first returning assembly?
explanation: Ezra 2:64 — 'The whole assembly together was 42,360, besides their male and female servants...'

content: According to Ezra 3:2, who was the priest and who was the governor leading the work of building the altar?
explanation: Ezra 3:2 — 'Then arose Jeshua the son of Jozadak, with his fellow priests, and Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel with his kinsmen, and they built the altar of the God of Israel...'
options: Jeshua son of Jozadak (priest) and Zerubbabel (governor)

content: According to Ezra 3:11-13, when the foundation of the temple was laid, how did the people respond?
explanation: Ezra 3:12-13 — 'But many of the priests and Levites and heads of fathers' houses, old men who had seen the first house, wept with a loud voice when they saw the foundation of this house being laid... while many shouted aloud for joy...' Older men wept remembering Solomon's temple; younger men rejoiced over the new beginning.
options: Old men wept loudly (remembering the first house), young men shouted for joy

content: According to Ezra 4:24, until what year and reign was the work on the temple halted?
explanation: Ezra 4:24 — 'Then the work on the house of God that is in Jerusalem stopped, and it ceased until the second year of the reign of Darius king of Persia.'
options: Second year of Darius king of Persia

content: According to Ezra 5:1, which two prophets prophesied to encourage the people to continue building?
explanation: Ezra 5:1 — 'Now the prophets, Haggai and Zechariah the son of Iddo, prophesied to the Jews who were in Judah and Jerusalem, in the name of the God of Israel who was over them.'

content: According to Ezra 6:15, in which year of Darius was the temple completed?
explanation: Ezra 6:15 — 'And this house was finished on the third day of the month of Adar, in the sixth year of the reign of Darius the king.' The Second Temple was completed about 516 BC, after roughly seventy years of ruin.

content: According to Ezra 6:19, what feast did the returning exiles celebrate after the temple was completed?
explanation: Ezra 6:19 — 'On the fourteenth day of the first month, the returned exiles kept the Passover.' The first feast in the new temple — commemorating the LORD's deliverance from Egypt, now applied to deliverance from Babylon.

content: According to Ezra 7:6, how is Ezra described?
explanation: Ezra 7:6 — '...this Ezra went up from Babylonia. He was a scribe skilled in the Law of Moses that the LORD, the God of Israel, had given, and the king granted him all that he asked, for the hand of the LORD his God was on him.'
options: A scribe skilled in the Law of Moses
tags: Scribe skilled in Law of Moses

content: According to Ezra 7:9, why was Ezra's journey successful?
explanation: Ezra 7:9 — '...for the good hand of his God was on him.' The phrase 'the good hand of God' is repeated several times in the book.
options: Because a large Persian army escorted him
Because the good hand of his God was on him
Because all neighboring nations were friendly

content: According to Ezra 7:10, what three things had Ezra set his heart to do?
explanation: Ezra 7:10 — 'For Ezra had set his heart to study the Law of the LORD, and to do it and to teach his statutes and rules in Israel.' Study, practice, then teach — the essential order.
options: Study the Law of the LORD, do it, and teach in Israel
Resist enemies, build the city, appoint commanders

content: According to Ezra 8:21, where did Ezra proclaim a fast before setting out?
explanation: Ezra 8:21 — 'Then I proclaimed a fast there, at the river Ahava, that we might humble ourselves before our God, to seek from him a safe journey for ourselves, our children, and all our goods.'

content: According to Ezra 8:22, why was Ezra ashamed to ask the king for soldiers?
explanation: Ezra 8:22 — '...for I was ashamed to ask the king for a band of soldiers and horsemen to protect us against the enemy on our way, since we had told the king, "The hand of our God is for good on all who seek him, and the power of his wrath is against all who forsake him."'
options: Because he had told the king the hand of God is for good on all who seek Him
Because he feared the king might suspect his loyalty
Because he had no money to pay soldiers
Because he had no authority to ask

content: According to Ezra 9:3, when Ezra heard about the foreign marriages, how did he react?
explanation: Ezra 9:3 — 'As soon as I heard this, I tore my garment and my cloak and pulled hair from my head and beard and sat appalled.'
options: Tore his garment, pulled hair from his head and beard, sat appalled
Held a feast with the elders

content: According to Ezra 9:6, how did Ezra begin his prayer of intercession?
explanation: Ezra 9:6 — '...O my God, I am ashamed and blush to lift my face to you, my God, for our iniquities have risen higher than our heads, and our guilt has mounted up to the heavens.'
options: O Lord, why have You let this happen?
O my God, I am ashamed and blush to lift my face — our iniquities have risen above our heads

content: According to Ezra 10:1, who gathered to Ezra in great number while he was praying?
explanation: Ezra 10:1 — 'While Ezra prayed and made confession, weeping and casting himself down before the house of God, a very great assembly of men, women, and children, gathered to him out of Israel...'
options: Men, women, and children — a very great assembly
tags: Great assembly of men, women, children

content: According to Ezra 10:11, what is the people called to do to set right their sin?
explanation: Ezra 10:11 — '...make confession to the LORD, the God of your fathers, and do his will. Separate yourselves from the peoples of the land and from the foreign wives.'
options: Confess, do God's will, separate from the peoples of the land and foreign wives
Travel to high places to pray

content: According to Ezra 1:2-3, what does Cyrus's decree include? (select all that apply)
explanation: Ezra 1:2-3 — '...The LORD, the God of heaven, has given me all the kingdoms of the earth, and he has charged me to build him a house at Jerusalem... Whoever is among you of all his people... let him go up to Jerusalem... and rebuild the house of the LORD...' Three core elements; 'abolishing the law of Moses' is not in the decree.
options: Declares that the LORD charged Cyrus with building the temple
Permits the people of Israel to return to Jerusalem
Encourages them to rebuild the house of the LORD

content: According to Ezra 6:14-15, by what factors was the Second Temple completed? (select all that apply)
explanation: Ezra 6:14-15 — '...the elders of the Jews built and prospered through the prophesying of Haggai the prophet and Zechariah the son of Iddo. They finished their building by decree of the God of Israel and by decree of Cyrus and Darius and Artaxerxes king of Persia...' 'Aid of the god Bel' is contrary.
options: Decree of the God of Israel
Decree of Cyrus, Darius, and Artaxerxes
Aid of the Babylonian god Bel

content: According to Ezra 7:10, what three things in order had Ezra set his heart to do? (select all that apply)
explanation: Ezra 7:10 — 'For Ezra had set his heart to study the Law of the LORD, and to do it and to teach his statutes and rules in Israel.' Three steps in order: study, practice, teach. 'Punish offenders immediately' is not the goal here.
options: Study the Law of the LORD

content: According to Ezra 9:1-2, from which peoples had Israel failed to separate? (select all that apply)
explanation: Ezra 9:1-2 — '...the people of Israel and the priests and the Levites have not separated themselves from the peoples of the lands with their abominations, from the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Jebusites, the Ammonites, the Moabites, the Egyptians, and the Amorites.' 'The Pharisees' is a later Jewish sect, not in the list.
